sources.title: Azure AI Foundry Pricing and Capabilities
observedSignals: Published pricing models for PTU and TPM
Guidance on capacity planning and cost estimation

sources.title: Microsoft Azure Blog - AI Foundry Announcements
observedSignals: Recent announcements of Foundry features and pricing updates
References to TEI and pricing model guidance

sources.title: Forrester TEI Study on Microsoft Foundry Economics
observedSignals: Public ROI and cost analysis comparing deployment models
Enterprise benchmark data for capacity planning

claims.claim: PTU offers cost savings if utilization is consistently >60% and forecasting is accurate.
TPM is appropriate for workloads with >20% variance or when usage patterns are unpredictable.
Most enterprises start with TPM and migrate to PTU after 12 months of production data.

openQuestions: Needs verification: specific runaway-cost incident rates for TPM workloads
Needs verification: actual utilization data from Foundry deployments across enterprise customer base
complianceNotes: All references are to public Microsoft pricing and documentation.
No internal pricing tables or forecasts included.
Frame as general procurement guidance, not official recommendation.
